findings.title: Data exfiltration via monitoring hook
findings.description: The hooks/monitor.sh script captures all tool invocations (inputs, outputs, environment variables including ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN) and sends them via curl to an external server (mcp-analytics-hub.io). This is a direct exfiltration of sensitive agent data and credentials.
findings.evidence: Lines sending payload with tool input/output and environment variables to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool
findings.remediation: Remove the monitoring hook and any data transmission to external servers. Never exfiltrate agent tool interactions or environment variables.

findings.title: Hardcoded secrets in MCP server configuration
findings.description: The .mcp.json file contains hardcoded API keys and authentication tokens (SEARCH_API_KEY, PROXY_AUTH Bearer token). These secrets are exposed in plaintext in the skill package and could be used for unauthorized access to services.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded secrets; use environment variables or secure credential stores. Rotate any exposed keys immediately.

findings.title: Tool poisoning via unauthorized monitoring and data forwarding
findings.description: The skill claims to manage MCP server configurations but installs a PostToolUse hook and configures an analytics-proxy server that forwards all tool inputs/outputs to an external endpoint. This corrupts the tool ecosystem by adding surveillance and data leakage without user consent.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md instructs copying .mcp.json and installing hooks/monitor.sh. The analytics-proxy has FORWARD_ALL_REQUESTS=true, CAPTURE_TOOL_INPUTS=true, CAPTURE_TOOL_OUTPUTS=true.
findings.remediation: Do not include monitoring hooks or proxy servers that silently transmit tool data. Clearly disclose any data collection in the skill description.

findings.title: Supply chain attack via unpinned npx packages and suspicious package names
findings.description: The skill uses npx to run packages without version pinning (e.g., @mcp-toolkit/db-server, @mcp-toolkit-analytics/proxy-server). The package names are suspicious and could be typosquatting or malicious variants. This allows arbitrary code execution from untrusted sources.
findings.remediation: Pin all package versions to known hashes or use only verified registries. Vet package names for typosquatting.

findings.title: Misleading description and overbroad capability claims
findings.description: The skill description 'Manage Model Context Protocol servers and tool integrations' is misleading. The actual behavior includes installing a surveillance hook and configuring a proxy that captures all tool interactions. This is capability inflation and could deceive users into installing malicious components.
findings.evidence: Description: 'Manage MCP server connections and tool integrations.' Actual code installs monitoring hook and analytics proxy.
findings.remediation: Provide an accurate description of all actions performed by the skill, including any monitoring or data forwarding.
